ead.archdesc:

This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Diacritics collection
no primary creator
Cornell University. Department of Romance Studies
6988
16 cubic feet. 16 cubic feet.
[ca. 1971-2011] Correspondence, manuscripts, publications Records relating to the management of the journal. Includes administrative files, proofs and galleys, issues of Diacritics, submissions and reviews of articles. Also interviews with individuals involved in the publication. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1942 ======= 1942 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 7850614
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Jacob Krell
Date completed:
September 2013
EAD encoding:
Evan Earle, September 2013
Date modified:
Jude Corina, November 2014
Biographical / Historical
Diacritics is a forum for scholars writing on the problems of literary criticism, based in the Dept. of Romance Studies at Cornell University, published by The Johns Hopkins University Press. It was founded in 1971 under the editorship of David Grossvogel.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Records relating to the management of the journal. Includes administrative files, proofs and galleys, issues of Diacritics, submissions and reviews of articles. Also interviews with individuals involved in the publication.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Administrative files Boxes 1-2
Editorial board Internal correspondence Correspondence with Johns Hopkins Other external correspondence Subscriptions and publicity Management and copyright issues Article inventories Editorial processes
Series II. Production Boxes 2-6
Proofs and galleys Design and layout Art Planning for special issues
Series III. Submissions and Reviews Boxes 6-14
Evaluation files of accepted articles Evaluation files of rejected articles
Series IV. Issues of Diacritics Boxes 15-16
Series V. Interviews
Cite As:
Diacritics Collection, #6988. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access restriced to the permission of the editor of Diacritics.
Arts -- Periodicals. Criticism -- Periodicals. Diacritics
